title: bridelia curtisii
intro: Bridelia curtisii, also known as the "Uluh" or "Maham" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues, among others. Bridelia curtisii can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
